user.title: Home Assistant MCP Server Custom Component
user.description: This integration provides the advanced file and YAML configuration services used by ha-mcp. No Home Assistant Supervisor was detected, so the MCP Server add-on can't be installed automatically here. You can instead run the MCP server inside Home Assistant (recommended), or run it separately via Docker or pip.

data.embedded_server_enabled: Run the MCP server inside Home Assistant

data_description.embedded_server_enabled: Runs the full ha-mcp server in-process and exposes it through a Home Assistant webhook for remote access. You can change this later in the integration options.

addon.title: Install the Home Assistant MCP Server add-on
addon.description: Home Assistant Supervisor was detected. This integration can add the add-on repository and install and start the **Home Assistant MCP Server** add-on for you — no need to add a repository URL by hand. Leave the box checked to install it now, or uncheck to set up only the integration services.

data.install_addon: Install the Home Assistant MCP Server add-on

install_failed.title: Add-on installation failed

progress.install_addon: Adding the add-on repository, installing the Home Assistant MCP Server add-on, and starting it. This can take a few minutes.

abort.already_configured: The Home Assistant MCP Server Custom Component is already set up.

init.title: In-process MCP server
init.description:
Run the full ha-mcp server inside Home Assistant and expose it remotely through a Home Assistant webhook (reachable via Nabu Casa or any reverse proxy). All settings below apply only when the server is enabled.

{connect_url}

data.embedded_server_enabled: Run the MCP server inside Home Assistant
data.server_url: Home Assistant URL for the server (advanced)

data_description.server_port: Local TCP port the in-process server listens on. Defaults to 9584 so it can coexist with the MCP Server add-on.
data_description.bind_host: 127.0.0.1 keeps the server loopback-only (remote access is via the webhook). 0.0.0.0 additionally allows direct access from your LAN.
data_description.webhook_auth: none: the secret webhook URL is the credential (default). ha_auth: clients sign in with your Home Assistant account.
data_description.pip_spec: Pip requirement installed at runtime. Leave as the default unless testing a pre-release (for example a GitHub tarball URL).
data_description.server_url: How the in-process server reaches Home Assistant. The loopback default works for almost everyone.

embedded_server_start_failed.title: The in-process MCP server failed to start

embedded_package_install_failed.title: The in-process MCP server package could not be installed
